# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: microservices-peer-auth
  namespace: microservices
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: microservices-peer-auth
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: service-mesh
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: istio
    migration-phase: backward-compatible
  annotations:
    description: "PeerAuthentication policy allowing both plaintext and mTLS traffic during service mesh migration"
    migration-notes: "This PERMISSIVE mode policy should be updated to STRICT once all services are migrated to the mesh"
spec:
  # Apply to all workloads in the microservices namespace
  mtls:
    mode: PERMISSIVE
  # Port-level overrides for specific services if needed
  portLevelMtls:
    # Health check ports typically need plaintext access
    8080:
      mode: PERMISSIVE
    # Metrics/prometheus scraping port
    9090:
      mode: PERMISSIVE
    # gRPC communication port
    50051:
      mode: PERMISSIVE
